name: Russia
summary: Day 73-74 (Mon-Tue). **B2 RUSSIA AS POTENTIAL HEU CUSTODIAN NOW IMPLICIT IN IRAN'S 14-POINT COUNTER-PROPOSAL (REPORTED via WSJ primary): Iran offered to dilute portion of HEU under IAEA supervision + transfer remainder to A THIRD COUNTRY, but with explicit RETURN CLAUSE if US exits any future deal.** No party publicly named Russia as the custodian candidate, but Putin's standing offer (May 9) is the implicit candidate per market analyst read. **US has not confirmed the uranium-transfer detail's specific custodian — Russia formally sidelined but operationally available.** **TRUMP REJECTED IRAN'S RESPONSE AS 'TOTALLY UNACCEPTABLE'** — so the HEU-transfer-with-return-clause is now in rejection state; if any framework is salvaged, Russia-custodian path remains open. **B3 TRUMP WH SIT-ROOM MAY 11 considering renewed strikes; Trump China visit May 14-15 = forced pause window** — Russia not at the table in Beijing. Pravda (Russian state) published the May 10 Doha bulk-carrier-strike attribution to 'two Iranian UAVs' — UNCONFIRMED by Western forensics; Russian-state single-side adversarial chain. TASS/Pravda SILENT on the WSJ UAE-Lavan revelation (likely because UAE-as-third-combatant complicates Russia's preferred US-Israel-vs-Iran framing). Russian oil flow to Indian refiners ~1.9 mbpd preserved under 30-day waiver. Uranium URA +3.7% 1D / +12.3% 1M — Cameco/Kazatomprom proxy bid stays strong on Iran HEU concession + AI-power demand. PRIOR FRAMING (preserved): Russia retains rhetorical support for Iran via state media + UNSC veto + Bushehr two new nuclear units commitment. **The HEU-custodian path is the most consequential structural opening for Russia in cycle — if Iran's response is salvaged (post-Beijing) and Russia is formally named as HEU custodian, Russia's strategic position vis-à-vis nuclear file regulation strengthens materially.** The 14-point MOU's 'Iran ships out highly-enriched uranium stockpile' provision necessarily requires a custodian — if not Russia, then potentially the IAEA, Kazakhstan (mentioned in earlier-cycle reporting), or US-based facility. Peskov reaffirmed via Pravda EN April 18 (and reiterated to India Today early April) that Russia's offer to take Iran's enriched uranium 'remains active' but the US has rejected the proposal. JPost specifically described the MOU provision as 'Tehran give away enriched uranium' (custodian undefined). The Pakistan track has displaced Russia as the operational mediator. **The 21-day formal-proposal window from prior brief (RESOLVED-NO at Day 9) needs reframing: Russia is sidelined as mediator, BUT could be reactivated as uranium custodian — different role and remains live.** Russia retains structural facets: rhetorical support for Iran via state media (Pravda EN, TASS), intelligence support via permanent communications channel, UNSC veto, Bushehr two new nuclear units commitment. Russian-oil waiver (30-day India extension) preserves ~1.9 mbpd Russian flow to Indian refiners. URA +7.5% partly reflects HEU custodian path legitimization (Cameco 24% of URA, AI power demand intact). Pravda Trump May 6 ran 'IDF preparing large-scale Iran op' single-source claim — REPORTED only, NOT verified mainstream Western media; Russian adversarial-media chain.

active_triggers.condition: Russia announces formal proposal as nuclear-material custodian within 14 days of Putin-Araghchi meeting (by 2026-05-11)

historical_summaries.summary: 2 signals. Russia providing intelligence to Iran including data on US military positions

10 signals. Russia remains an intelligence/diplomatic supporter of Iran | Russia friendly-flag in Iran's three-tier Hormuz system per Bloomberg Tracker — vessels flagged to Russia continue to transit | Russia and China vetoed Bahrain's UNSC resolution to keep Hormuz open (April 7, 11-2 vote, France abstained)
